violations.description: Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Sanitizing solution in bucket for holding wiping cloths was found at 0ppm, chlorine. Chlorine solutions for wiping cloths should be at least 100ppm. **This was corrected.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Food contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in good repair or sound condition. Lid for the ice machine bin was found with insulation exposed. This should be repaired and maintained in good repair.--Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. Counter that the Coke machine is on was found leaning to one side. Counters should be maintained in good repair.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Blade for commercial can opener was found dirty during inspection. This should be cleaned and maintained clean.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Shelving throughout the kitchen and walk-in cooler were found dirty. Equipment was found dirty, inside and out, throughout the kitchen. These items should be cleaned and maintained clean.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Hood ventilation system was found dirty during inspection. This should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors and walls throughout the kitchen were found dirty. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

General premises. — --Observed traffic of unauthorized person through food preparation, food storage or warewashing area. Observed person enter the building and entered the server area and helped himself to two beverages before leaving. Only authorized individuals should be allowed in food preparation areas.

General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed food employee wipe his hands on towel after putting food on the flat top grill. Hands should be washed with soap and water instead of wiping on the towel.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Raw chicken and green peppers were found stored on the floor of the walk-in cooler. Food items should be stored at least 6 inches off of the floor.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Hard boiled eggs and diced potatoes were found in the prep table without date markings. Potentially hazardous foods should be marked with date so they can be discarded properly.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. Counter that the Coke machine is on was found leaning to one side. Counters should be maintained in good repair.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Table top griddle on shelving in storage area was found dirty. This should be cleaned after each use before storing.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Shelving throughout the kitchen and walk-in cooler were found dirty. Equipment was found dirty, inside and out, throughout the kitchen. These items should be cleaned and maintained clean.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Ventilation system filters were found dirty during inspection. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. Clean utensils found stored in a dirty bowl. Utensils should be stored in a clean location.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Flooring throughout the kitchen, especially under equipment, was found dirty. Flooring should be cleaned more thoroughly.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. Flooring throughout the kitchen was found missing and in poor repair. Flooring must be maintained in good repair to allow for an easily cleanable surface.
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
